heading: Mandatory recall authority
label: 364g Mandatory recall authority
notes: Construction; ConfidentialityNothing in , which enacted this section, to be construed to authorize the disclosure of information that is prohibited from disclosure under or or that is subject to withholding under , see , set out as a note under .section 3502 of Pub. L. 117–328section 331(j) of this titlesection 1905 of title 18section 552(b)(4) of title 5section 3503(c)(2) of Pub. L. 117–328section 364 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that there is a reasonable probability that a cosmetic is adulterated under or misbranded under and the use of or exposure to such cosmetic will cause serious adverse health consequences or death, the Secretary shall provide the responsible person with an opportunity to voluntarily cease distribution and recall such article. If the responsible person refuses to or does not voluntarily cease distribution or recall such cosmetic within the time and manner prescribed by the Secretary (if so prescribed), the Secretary may, by order, require, as the Secretary determines necessary, such person to immediately cease distribution of such article.section 361 of this titlesection 362 of this title

children.heading: Hearing
children.label: b Hearing
children.text: The Secretary shall provide the responsible person who is subject to an order under subsection (a) with an opportunity for an informal hearing, to be held not later than 10 days after the date of issuance of the order, on whether adequate evidence exists to justify the order.
vacate the order, if the Secretary determines that inadequate grounds exist to support the actions required by the order;
continue the order ceasing distribution of the cosmetic until a date specified in such order; or

amend the order to require a recall of the cosmetic, including any requirements to notify appropriate persons, a timetable for the recall to occur, and a schedule for updates to be provided to the Secretary regarding such recall.

children.heading: Action following order
children.label: d Action following order
children.text: Any person who is subject to an order pursuant to paragraph (2) or (3) of subsection (c) shall immediately cease distribution of or recall, as applicable, the cosmetic and provide notification as required by such order.

children.heading: Notice to persons affected
children.label: e Notice to persons affected
children.text: If the Secretary determines necessary, the Secretary may require the person subject to an order pursuant to subsection (a) or an amended order pursuant to paragraph (2) or (3) of subsection (c) to provide either a notice of a recall order for, or an order to cease distribution of, such cosmetic, as applicable, under this section to appropriate persons, including persons who manufacture, distribute, import, or offer for sale such product that is the subject of an order and to the public.

children.label: A
children.text: of the recall to consumers and retailers to whom such cosmetic was, or may have been, distributed; and

children.label: i
children.text: the name of the cosmetic subject to the recall;

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of the risk associated with such article; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to the extent practicable, information for consumers about similar cosmetics that are not affected by the recall; and
ensure publication, as appropriate, on the website of the Food and Drug Administration of an image of the cosmetic that is the subject of the press release described in paragraph (1), if available.

children.heading: No delegation
children.label: g No delegation
children.text: The authority conferred by this section to order a recall or vacate a recall order shall not be delegated to any officer or employee other than the Commissioner.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: h Effect
children.text: Nothing in this section shall affect the authority of the Secretary to request or participate in a voluntary recall, or to issue an order to cease distribution or to recall under any other provision of this subchapter.
